mechanism_narrative: A persistently elevated federal funds rate increases the equity risk premium and discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate signals weakening aggregate demand and impending earnings downgrades, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in greater than 50% of instances when the trigger fires.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising consumer prices and a restrictive federal funds rate interact to increase real discount rates and compress profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or the negative sign flips in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: During early-cycle recovery, accelerating retail sales signal strengthening consumer demand and revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS-driven 5-day signals show a hit ratio below 52% or negative mean forward return during early-cycle recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly increases in industrial production confirm manufacturing momentum and capacity utilization gains, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts reflect durable demand strength and residential investment expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in nonfarm payrolls signals rising household income and aggregate demand support, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS surprises are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of months.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating consumer price index erodes real corporate earnings expectations and pressures valuation multiples, causing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns when inflation rises briskly.
failure_condition: Month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases above 0.5% are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.
